A system that automatically generates scientific architecture diagrams from text with high semantic fidelity can be useful in multiple applications like enterprise architecture visualization, AI-driven software design, and educational content creation. Hence, in this paper, we focus on leveraging language models to perform semantic understanding of the input text description to generate intermediate code that can be processed to generate high-fidelity architecture diagrams. Unfortunately, no clean large-scale open-access dataset exists, implying lack of any effective open models for this task. Hence, we contribute a comprehensive dataset, \system, comprising scientific architecture images, their corresponding textual descriptions, and associated DOT code representations. Leveraging this resource, we fine-tune a suite of small language models, and also perform in-context learning using GPT-4o. Through extensive experimentation, we show that \system{} models significantly outperform existing baseline models like DiagramAgent and perform at par with in-context learning-based generations from GPT-4o. We make the code, data and models publicly available.
title_zh: Text2Arch：一个从自然语言描述生成科学架构图的数据集
abstract_zh: 仅通过文本传达复杂系统设计或科学过程既低效又容易产生歧义。能够从文本中自动生成语义高度一致的科学架构图的系统，在企业架构可视化、AI驱动的软件设计以及教育内容创作等多个领域都具有重要应用价值。因此，本文聚焦于利用语言模型对输入的文本描述进行语义理解，生成可被处理为高保真架构图的中间代码。然而，目前尚无干净的大规模开源数据集可用，导致该任务缺乏有效的公开模型。为此，我们构建了一个全面的数据集 \system，包含科学架构图像、对应的文本描述以及相关的DOT代码表示。基于这一资源，我们对一系列小型语言模型进行了微调，并采用GPT-4o进行上下文学习（in-context learning）。通过大量实验，我们证明了 \system{} 模型显著优于现有基线模型（如DiagramAgent），且在性能上与基于GPT-4o的上下文学习生成结果相当。相关代码、数据和模型均已公开发布。

title: Serving Chain-structured Jobs with Large Memory Footprints with Application to Large Foundation Model Serving
authors: Tingyang Sun, Ting He, I-Hong Hou
abstract: As a current trend in Artificial Intelligence (AI), large foundation models are increasingly employed as the core of AI services. However, even after training, serving such models at scale remains a challenging task due to their heavy resource footprints, particularly in terms of GPU memory. While recent works revealed unique characteristics of systems serving foundation models that distinguish them from traditional distributed computing systems, there is still a lack of fundamental understanding of the underlying system management problems. This work aims at addressing this gap by extracting a novel problem of "server chain composition" via block placement and cache allocation for serving chainstructured jobs with large memory footprints, which models a fundamental problem in serving large foundation models through pipeline parallelism. After showing the NP-hardness of the optimal solution, the focus is turned to developing scalable algorithms with guaranteed performance under state-of-the-art load balancing. Application of the proposed solution to a distributed large language model (LLM) serving system shows significant reduction of response times compared to state-of-the-art solutions.
title_zh: 具有大内存占用的链式结构任务的调度——以大型基础模型服务为例
abstract_zh: 作为人工智能（AI）领域的一项当前趋势，大型基础模型正越来越多地被用作AI服务的核心。然而，即使在完成训练后，大规模部署这些模型仍面临巨大挑战，主要因其巨大的资源开销，尤其是在GPU内存方面。尽管近期研究揭示了服务于基础模型的系统所具有的独特特性，这些特性使其区别于传统的分布式计算系统，但对底层系统管理问题的基本理解仍然不足。本文旨在填补这一空白，通过块放置与缓存分配策略，提出一个新颖的“服务器链组合”问题，用于处理具有大内存占用的流水线结构化任务，该问题建模了通过流水线并行方式服务大型基础模型时所面临的根本性挑战。在证明最优解为NP难问题后，研究重点转向开发在先进负载均衡条件下具备性能保障的可扩展算法。将所提出的解决方案应用于分布式大语言模型（LLM）服务系统，结果显示其相比现有最先进方案显著降低了响应时间。

title: Prism: Symbolic Superoptimization of Tensor Programs
authors: Mengdi Wu, Xiaoyu Jiang, Oded Padon, Zhihao Jia
abstract: This paper presents Prism, the first symbolic superoptimizer for tensor programs. The key idea is sGraph, a symbolic, hierarchical representation that compactly encodes large classes of tensor programs by symbolically representing some execution parameters. Prism organizes optimization as a two-level search: it constructs symbolic graphs that represent families of programs, and then instantiates them into concrete implementations. This formulation enables structured pruning of provably suboptimal regions of the search space using symbolic reasoning over operator semantics, algebraic identities, and hardware constraints. We develop techniques for efficient symbolic graph generation, equivalence verification via e-graph rewriting, and parameter instantiation through auto-tuning. Together, these components allow Prism to bridge the rigor of exhaustive search with the scalability required for modern ML workloads. Evaluation on five commonly used LLM workloads shows that Prism achieves up to $2.2\times$ speedup over best superoptimizers and $4.9\times$ over best compiler-based approaches, while reducing end-to-end optimization time by up to $3.4\times$.
title_zh: 棱镜：张量程序的符号超优化
abstract_zh: 本文提出了Prism，这是首个针对张量程序的符号超优化器。其核心思想是sGraph——一种符号化、分层的表示方法，通过符号化地表示某些执行参数，紧凑地编码了大量张量程序。Prism将优化过程组织为两级搜索：首先构建代表程序族的符号图，然后将其实例化为具体的实现。这种形式化方法使得我们能够利用对算子语义、代数恒等式以及硬件约束的符号推理，系统性地剪枝掉明显次优的搜索空间区域。我们开发了高效生成符号图的技术，通过e-图重写进行等价性验证，并通过自动调优完成参数实例化。这些组件共同作用，使Prism在保持穷尽搜索严谨性的同时，具备满足现代机器学习工作负载所需的良好可扩展性。在五个常用的大型语言模型（LLM）工作负载上的评估表明，与最佳超优化器相比，Prism实现了最高达2.2倍的加速；与最佳编译器方法相比，最高提速达4.9倍；同时，端到端优化时间最多减少了3.4倍。

title: Enhancing Large Language Models with Retrieval Augmented Generation for Software Testing and Inspection Automation
authors: Zoe Fingleton, Nazanin Siavash, Armin Moin
abstract: In this paper, we focus on automating two of the widely used Verification and Validation (V&V) activities in the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC): Software testing and software inspection (also known as review). Concerning the former, we concentrate on automated test case generation using Large Language Models (LLMs). For the latter, we enable inspection of the source code by LLMs. To address the known LLM hallucination problem, in which LLMs confidently produce incorrect outputs, we implement a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline to integrate supplementary knowledge sources and provide additional context to the LLM. Our experimental results indicate that incorporating external context via the RAG pipeline has a generally positive impact on both test case generation and code inspection. This novel approach reduces the total project cost by saving human testers'/inspectors' time. It also improves the effectiveness and efficiency of these V&V activities, as evidenced by our experimental study.
title_zh: 利用检索增强生成技术提升大型语言模型在软件测试与检查自动化中的应用
abstract_zh: 本文聚焦于自动化软件开发生命周期（SDLC）中广泛使用的两项验证与确认（V&V）活动：软件测试和软件审查（亦称代码评审）。针对前者，我们重点研究利用大语言模型（LLMs）实现自动化测试用例生成；针对后者，我们探索由大语言模型执行源代码审查的可行性。为应对大语言模型普遍存在但令人担忧的“幻觉”问题——即模型自信地生成错误输出——我们引入了检索增强生成（RAG）管道，将外部知识源整合进模型，并为其提供额外上下文信息。实验结果表明，通过RAG管道引入外部上下文对测试用例生成和代码审查均具有普遍积极影响。这一创新方法显著降低了项目总成本，节省了人工测试人员和审查人员的时间，同时在有效性与效率方面也明显提升了这两项V&V活动的表现，相关结论得到了实验研究的充分支持。

title: Compressing Sequences in the Latent Embedding Space: $K$-Token Merging for Large Language Models
authors: Zihao Xu, John Harvill, Ziwei Fan, Yizhou Sun, Hao Ding, Hao Wang
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) incur significant computational and memory costs when processing long prompts, as full self-attention scales quadratically with input length. Token compression aims to address this challenge by reducing the number of tokens representing inputs. However, existing prompt-compression approaches primarily operate in token space and overlook inefficiencies in the latent embedding space. In this paper, we propose K-Token Merging, a latent-space compression framework that merges each contiguous block of K token embeddings into a single embedding via a lightweight encoder. The compressed sequence is processed by a LoRA-adapted LLM, while generation remains in the original vocabulary. Experiments on structural reasoning (Textualized Tree), sentiment classification (Amazon Reviews), and code editing (CommitPackFT) show that K-Token Merging lies on the Pareto frontier of performance vs. compression, achieving up to 75% input length reduction with minimal performance degradation.
title_zh: 在潜在嵌入空间中压缩序列：面向大型语言模型的K-Token合并方法
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理长提示（prompt）时会面临显著的计算和内存开销，因为完整的自注意力机制的复杂度随输入长度呈二次方增长。令牌压缩（token compression）旨在通过减少表示输入的令牌数量来缓解这一挑战。然而，现有的提示压缩方法主要在令牌空间中操作，忽略了潜在嵌入空间中的效率问题。本文提出了一种名为 K-Token Merging 的潜在空间压缩框架，该框架通过一个轻量级编码器将每连续 K 个令牌嵌入合并为单一嵌入。压缩后的序列由经过 LoRA 适配的 LLM 处理，而生成过程仍保持在原始词汇表中。在结构化推理（Textualized Tree）、情感分类（Amazon Reviews）以及代码编辑（CommitPackFT）任务上的实验表明，K-Token Merging 位于性能与压缩率之间的帕累托前沿，能够在实现高达 75% 输入长度缩减的同时，仅带来极小的性能损失。

title: Feedback-Driven Execution for LLM-Based Binary Analysis
authors: XiangRui Zhang, Qiang Li, Haining Wang
abstract: Binary analysis increasingly relies on large language models (LLMs) to perform semantic reasoning over complex program behaviors. However, existing approaches largely adopt a one-pass execution paradigm, where reasoning operates over a fixed program representation constructed by static analysis tools. This formulation limits the ability to adapt exploration based on intermediate results and makes it difficult to sustain long-horizon, multi-path analysis under constrained context. We present FORGE, a system that rethinks LLM-based analysis as a feedback-driven execution process. FORGE interleaves reasoning and tool interaction through a reasoning-action-observation loop, enabling incremental exploration and evidence construction. To address the instability of long-horizon reasoning, we introduce a Dynamic Forest of Agents (FoA), a decomposed execution model that dynamically coordinates parallel exploration while bounding per-agent context. We evaluate FORGE on 3,457 real-world firmware binaries. FORGE identifies 1,274 vulnerabilities across 591 unique binaries, achieving 72.3% precision while covering a broader range of vulnerability types than prior approaches. These results demonstrate that structuring LLM-based analysis as a decomposed, feedback-driven execution system enables both scalable reasoning and high-quality outcomes in long-horizon tasks.
title_zh: 基于反馈驱动的LLM二进制分析执行
abstract_zh: 二进制分析越来越多地依赖大型语言模型（LLMs）来对复杂的程序行为进行语义推理。然而，现有方法大多采用单次执行范式，即推理基于静态分析工具构建的固定程序表示。这种模式限制了根据中间结果动态调整探索路径的能力，并使得在上下文受限的情况下难以实现长周期、多路径分析。我们提出了 FORGE，一个重新思考 LLM 基于分析的反馈驱动执行过程的系统。FORGE 通过“推理—行动—观察”循环，将推理与工具交互交织在一起，从而实现增量式探索和证据构建。为应对长周期推理中的不稳定性，我们引入了动态代理森林（Dynamic Forest of Agents, FoA），这是一种分解式的执行模型，能够动态协调并行探索，同时控制每个代理的上下文范围。我们在 3,457 个真实世界固件二进制文件上评估了 FORGE，成功识别出 591 个唯一二进制文件中的 1,274 个漏洞，达到 72.3% 的精确率，且覆盖的漏洞类型比以往方法更为广泛。这些结果表明，将 LLM 基于分析结构化为一种分解式、反馈驱动的执行系统，能够在长周期任务中实现可扩展的推理与高质量的结果。

title: Prefill-as-a-Service: KVCache of Next-Generation Models Could Go Cross-Datacenter
authors: Ruoyu Qin, Weiran He, Yaoyu Wang, Zheming Li, Xinran Xu, Yongwei Wu, Weimin Zheng, Mingxing Zhang
abstract: Prefill-decode (PD) disaggregation has become the standard architecture for large-scale LLM serving, but in practice its deployment boundary is still determined by KVCache transfer. In conventional dense-attention models, prefill generates huge KVCache traffics that keep prefill and decode tightly coupled within a single high-bandwidth network domain, limiting heterogeneous deployment and resource elasticity. Recent hybrid-attention architectures substantially reduce KVCache size, making cross-cluster KVCache transport increasingly plausible. However, smaller KVCache alone does not make heterogeneous cross-datacenter PD serving practical: real workloads remain bursty, request lengths are highly skewed, prefix caches are unevenly distributed, and inter-cluster bandwidth fluctuates. A naive design that fully externalizes prefill can therefore still suffer from congestion, unstable queueing, and poor utilization. We present Prefill-as-a-Service (PrfaaS), a cross-datacenter serving architecture that selectively offloads long-context prefill to standalone, compute-dense prefill clusters and transfers the resulting KVCache over commodity Ethernet to local PD clusters for decode. Rather than treating reduced KVCache as sufficient, PrfaaS combines model-side KV efficiency with system-side selective offloading, bandwidth-aware scheduling, and cache-aware request placement. This design removes the requirement that heterogeneous accelerators share the same low-latency RDMA fabric, enabling independent scaling of prefill and decode capacity across loosely coupled clusters. In a case study using an internal 1T-parameter hybrid model, a PrfaaS-augmented heterogeneous deployment achieves 54% and 32% higher serving throughput than homogeneous PD and naive heterogeneous baselines, respectively, while consuming only modest cross-datacenter bandwidth.
title_zh: 预填充即服务：下一代模型的KV缓存或将实现跨数据中心部署
abstract_zh:
预填充解码（Prefill-decode, PD）解耦架构已成为大规模大语言模型（LLM）服务的标准方案，但在实际部署中，其边界仍受限于KV缓存的传输。在传统的密集注意力模型中，预填充阶段会产生巨大的KV缓存流量，导致预填充与解码必须紧密耦合在同一高带宽网络域内，从而限制了异构部署和资源弹性扩展。近期提出的混合注意力架构显著减小了KV缓存规模，使跨集群KV缓存传输变得愈发可行。然而，仅靠缩小KV缓存并不能真正实现跨数据中心的异构PD服务：真实工作负载仍具有突发性，请求长度高度不均衡，前缀缓存分布不均，且跨集群带宽波动频繁。因此，若简单地将预填充完全外部化，仍可能导致网络拥塞、队列不稳定以及资源利用率低下。

为此，我们提出了一种名为“预填充即服务”（Prefill-as-a-Service, PrfaaS）的跨数据中心服务架构。该架构通过选择性地将长上下文的预填充任务卸载至独立的、计算密集型的预填充集群，并将生成的KV缓存通过普通以太网传输至本地的PD集群进行解码。PrfaaS并未仅仅依赖于减小KV缓存这一单一手段，而是结合了模型层面的KV缓存效率优化与系统层面的选择性卸载、带宽感知调度以及缓存感知的任务分配策略。这一设计彻底摆脱了异构加速器必须共享低延迟RDMA网络基础设施的限制，实现了预填充与解码能力在松耦合集群间的独立扩展。

在一项基于内部1万亿参数混合模型的案例研究中，采用PrfaaS增强的异构部署方案，相比同质化PD架构和简单的异构基线，分别实现了54%和32%的吞吐量提升，同时仅消耗适度的跨数据中心带宽。

title: COEVO: Co-Evolutionary Framework for Joint Functional Correctness and PPA Optimization in LLM-Based RTL Generation
authors: Heng Ping, Peiyu Zhang, Shixuan Li, Wei Yang, Anzhe Cheng, Shukai Duan, Xiaole Zhang, Paul Bogdan
abstract: LLM-based RTL code generation methods increasingly target both functional correctness and PPA quality, yet existing approaches universally decouple the two objectives, optimizing PPA only after correctness is fully achieved. Whether through sequential multi-agent pipelines, evolutionary search with binary correctness gates, or hierarchical reward dependencies, partially correct but architecturally promising candidates are systematically discarded. Moreover, existing methods reduce the multi-objective PPA space to a single scalar fitness, obscuring the trade-offs among area, delay, and power. To address these limitations, we propose COEVO, a co-evolutionary framework that unifies correctness and PPA optimization within a single evolutionary loop. COEVO formulates correctness as a continuous co-optimization dimension alongside area, delay, and power, enabled by an enhanced testbench that provides fine-grained scoring and detailed diagnostic feedback. An adaptive correctness gate with annealing allows PPA-promising but partially correct candidates to guide the search toward jointly optimal solutions. To preserve the full PPA trade-off structure, COEVO employs four-dimensional Pareto-based non-dominated sorting with configurable intra-level sorting, replacing scalar fitness without manual weight tuning. Evaluated on VerilogEval 2.0 and RTLLM 2.0, COEVO achieves 97.5\% and 94.5\% Pass@1 with GPT-5.4-mini, surpassing all agentic baselines across four LLM backbones, while attaining the best PPA on 43 out of 49 synthesizable RTLLM designs.
title_zh: COEVO：面向基于大语言模型的RTL生成中功能正确性与PPA协同优化的共进化框架
abstract_zh:
基于大语言模型（LLM）的RTL代码生成方法日益关注功能正确性与PPA（面积、功耗、延迟）性能的双重优化，但现有方法普遍将这两个目标解耦：仅在完全确保正确性后才开始优化PPA。无论是通过顺序多智能体流水线、带有二值正确性判据的进化搜索，还是层级奖励依赖机制，那些部分正确但具有架构潜力的候选方案均被系统性地淘汰。此外，现有方法将多目标PPA空间简化为单一标量适应度，掩盖了面积、延迟与功耗之间的权衡关系。

为解决上述局限，我们提出COEVO——一种协同进化框架，将正确性与PPA优化统一于同一进化循环中。COEVO将正确性作为与面积、延迟、功耗并列的连续协同优化维度，依托一个增强型测试平台，实现细粒度评分与详尽诊断反馈。引入具有退火特性的自适应正确性门控机制，使那些在PPA方面表现优异但尚未完全正确的候选方案仍能引导搜索过程，向联合最优解逼近。为保留完整的PPA权衡结构，COEVO采用四维帕累托非支配排序，并支持可配置的层内排序策略，替代传统标量适应度，无需手动调整权重。

在VerilogEval 2.0和RTLLM 2.0基准上的评估表明，使用GPT-5.4-mini时，COEVO分别达到97.5%和94.5%的Pass@1率，显著超越所有代理基线方法（涵盖四种LLM骨干网络），同时在49个可综合的RTLLM设计中，实现了43个的最佳PPA表现。

title: Discovering Novel LLM Experts via Task-Capability Coevolution
authors: Andrew Dai, Boris Meinardus, Ciaran Regan, Yingtao Tian, Yujin Tang
abstract: Frontier model developers aim to train models continually to possess emergent, diverse capabilities. To extend capabilities, the current pre-training and post-training paradigm requires manually starting training runs with static datasets or reward functions every time. Addressing this limitation, our work pursues the insight that open-endedness (via the coevolution of models and tasks) can discover models with increasingly novel skills in a single run. We introduce a new model development framework that extends coevolution to large language model (LLM) discovery, open-ended \textit{Assessment Coevolving with Diverse Capabilities} (AC/DC). AC/DC evolves both LLMs via model merging and natural language tasks via synthetic data generation. AC/DC discovers growing archives of LLMs that surpass the capabilities of larger LLMs while taking up less GPU memory. In particular, our LLM populations achieve a broader Coverage of expertise than other curated models or baselines on downstream benchmarks, without \textit{any} explicit benchmark optimization. Furthermore, AC/DC improves Coverage over time, continually innovates on tasks and models, and improves performance in multi-agent best-of-N selection. Our findings highlight the potential of coevolution as a means of discovering broader sets of capabilities from base LLMs. Overall, AC/DC brings us one step closer to a profoundly new paradigm of LLM development, where continual improvements to the diversity of model capabilities can be accelerated by leveraging existing models as stepping stones to increasingly powerful models.
title_zh: 通过任务-能力协同进化发现新型大模型专家
abstract_zh: 前沿模型开发者致力于持续训练模型，以使其具备涌现的、多样化的能力。然而，当前的预训练与后训练范式在扩展能力时，仍需手动启动训练任务，并依赖静态数据集或奖励函数。为解决这一局限性，我们的工作提出一个核心见解：通过模型与任务的共同进化实现开放性，可在单次运行中发现具有日益新颖技能的模型。为此，我们提出一种全新的模型开发框架——开放性评估与多样化能力协同进化（Assessment Coevolving with Diverse Capabilities, AC/DC），将共同进化机制拓展至大语言模型（LLM）的发现过程。AC/DC 通过模型合并演化大语言模型，并借助合成数据生成自然语言任务，实现双向进化。该框架成功发掘出不断增长的 LLM 资源库，其性能超越了许多更大规模的 LLM，同时占用更少的 GPU 内存。特别地，我们的 LLM 群体在下游基准测试中展现出比其他精心筛选的模型或基线方法更广泛的能力覆盖范围，且无需任何显式的基准优化。此外，AC/DC 随时间推移持续提升覆盖度，不断在任务和模型层面实现创新，并在多智能体“最佳 N 选一”选择机制中显著提升性能。研究结果凸显了共同进化作为从基础 LLM 中发掘更广泛能力集合的潜力。总体而言，AC/DC 推动我们向一种根本性的新范式迈进：通过利用现有模型作为跳板，持续加速模型能力多样性的提升，从而实现大语言模型发展的全新路径。

title: HWE-Bench: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Real-World Hardware Bug Repair Tasks
authors: Fan Cui, Hongyuan Hou, Zizhang Luo, Chenyun Yin, Yun Liang
abstract: Existing benchmarks for hardware design primarily evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) on isolated, component-level tasks such as generating HDL modules from specifications, leaving repository-scale evaluation unaddressed. We introduce HWE-Bench, the first large-scale, repository-level benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on real-world hardware bug repair tasks. HWE-Bench comprises 417 task instances derived from real historical bug-fix pull requests across six major open-source projects spanning both Verilog/SystemVerilog and Chisel, covering RISC-V cores, SoCs, and security roots-of-trust. Each task is grounded in a fully containerized environment where the agent must resolve a real bug report, with correctness validated through the project's native simulation and regression flows. The benchmark is built through a largely automated pipeline that enables efficient expansion to new repositories. We evaluate seven LLMs with four agent frameworks and find that the best agent resolves 70.7% of tasks overall, with performance exceeding 90% on smaller cores but dropping below 65% on complex SoC-level projects. We observe larger performance gaps across models than commonly reported on software benchmarks, and difficulty is driven by project scope and bug-type distribution rather than code size alone. Our failure analysis traces agent failures to three stages of the debugging process: fault localization, hardware-semantic reasoning, and cross-artifact coordination across RTL, configuration, and verification components, providing concrete directions for developing more capable hardware-aware agents.
title_zh: HWE-Bench：面向真实世界硬件缺陷修复任务的大型语言模型智能体评估基准
abstract_zh: 现有硬件设计领域的基准测试主要针对大型语言模型（LLMs）在孤立的、组件级任务上的表现进行评估，例如从规格说明生成硬件描述语言（HDL）模块，而未能涵盖代码库级别的综合评估。为此，我们提出了 HWE-Bench——首个大规模、基于代码库级别的基准测试，用于评估 LLM 代理在真实世界硬件缺陷修复任务中的能力。HWE-Bench 包含来自六个主流开源项目中真实历史缺陷修复拉取请求的 417 个任务实例，覆盖 Verilog/SystemVerilog 和 Chisel 两种语言，涉及 RISC-V 核心、系统级芯片（SoC）以及安全可信根等场景。每个任务均基于完全容器化的环境，要求代理解决真实的 bug 报告，并通过项目自身的仿真与回归测试流程验证结果正确性。该基准测试通过高度自动化的流水线构建，可高效扩展至新的代码库。我们对七种 LLM 模型搭配四种代理框架进行了评估，发现表现最佳的代理整体解决了 70.7% 的任务，在小型核心上性能超过 90%，但在复杂 SoC 级别项目中下降至 65% 以下。我们观察到不同模型之间的性能差距远大于以往软件基准测试中报告的情况，且难度主要由项目规模和缺陷类型分布决定，而非仅由代码量决定。我们的失败分析揭示了代理在调试过程中的三个关键失败阶段：故障定位、硬件语义推理，以及跨 RTL、配置和验证组件的协同协调问题，为开发更具硬件感知能力的智能代理提供了明确的研究方向。

title: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
authors: David Y. Y. Tan, Kellie Chin, Jingxian Zhang
abstract: We present AgentGA, a framework that evolves autonomous code-generation runs by optimizing the agent seed: the task prompt plus optional parent archives that initialize a fresh workspace. The outer loop searches over these reusable starting conditions rather than editing code directly. Each generation launches a fresh autonomous run from a reset workspace, while selected parent archives provide inherited artifacts that descendants can inspect and reuse. AgentGA couples a population-level genetic algorithm with long-horizon agents; selection uses deterministic 1:1 elite tournaments and operator allocation is adapted online with a modified Hedge controller. We instantiate the approach for tabular AutoML on the 16-competition Weco-Kaggle Lite benchmark. On the 10 benchmark runs reported here, AgentGA averages 74.52% Exceeds % of Human versus 54.15% for AIDE. Across 1135 parent-child comparisons, descendants given parent archives outperform runs started from scratch, indicating that inherited artifacts improve later autonomous runs. These findings support agent-seed optimization as a practical design point for autonomous code-search systems.
title_zh: AgentGA：在代理种子空间中进化代码解决方案
abstract_zh: 我们提出AgentGA，一种通过优化代理种子（即任务提示词，可选地包含父级归档文件以初始化全新工作空间）来演化自主代码生成过程的框架。与直接修改代码不同，外层循环搜索这些可复用的初始条件。每一代均从重置的工作空间启动一次全新的自主运行，而选定的父级归档文件则提供可供后代检查和复用的继承性成果。AgentGA将种群级别的遗传算法与长周期智能体相结合；选择机制采用确定性的1:1精英锦标赛，操作符分配则通过改进的Hedge控制器在线动态调整。我们在Weco-Kaggle Lite基准的16个竞赛任务中，针对表格型AutoML实例化了该方法。在本报告的10次基准测试运行中，AgentGA平均达到74.52%的“超越人类”表现，显著优于AIDE的54.15%。在1135组父子对比中，获得父级归档的后代表现优于从零开始的运行，表明继承性成果能够提升后续自主运行的效果。这些发现支持将代理种子优化作为自主代码搜索系统的一个实用设计方向。

title: AgileLog: A Forkable Shared Log for Agents on Data Streams
authors: Shreesha G. Bhat, Tony Hong, Michael Noguera, Ramnatthan Alagappan, Aishwarya Ganesan
abstract: In modern data-streaming systems, alongside traditional programs, a new type of entity has emerged that can interact with streaming data: AI agents. Unlike traditional programs, AI agents use LLM reasoning to accomplish high-level tasks specified in natural language over streaming data. Unfortunately, current streaming systems cannot fully support agents: they lack the fundamental mechanisms to avoid the performance interference caused by agentic tasks and to safely handle agentic writes. We argue that the shared log, the core abstraction underlying streaming data, must support creating forks of itself, and that such a forkable shared log serves as a great substrate for agents acting on streaming data. We propose AgileLog, a new shared log abstraction that provides novel forking primitives for agentic use cases. We design Bolt, an implementation of the AgileLog abstraction, that uses novel techniques to make forks cheap, and provide logical and performance isolation.
title_zh: AgileLog：面向数据流中代理的可分支共享日志
abstract_zh: 在现代数据流系统中，除了传统程序之外，一种新型实体——AI代理（AI agents）也应运而生，能够与流式数据进行交互。与传统程序不同，AI代理利用大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力，通过自然语言描述来完成高阶任务。然而，当前的数据流系统尚无法充分支持这类代理：它们缺乏必要的基础机制，以避免代理任务带来的性能干扰，并安全地处理代理写入操作。我们认为，作为流式数据核心抽象的共享日志（shared log），必须具备自身分叉的能力；而这种可分叉的共享日志，正是支持代理在流式数据上运行的理想基础架构。为此，我们提出了 AgileLog——一种全新的共享日志抽象，专为代理使用场景设计了创新的分叉原语。我们进一步设计并实现了 Bolt，作为 AgileLog 抽象的具体实现，采用新颖的技术手段使分叉操作成本极低，并提供逻辑与性能上的隔离保障。

title: MARS$^2$: Scaling Multi-Agent Tree Search via Reinforcement Learning for Code Generation
authors: Pengfei Li, Shijie Wang, Fangyuan Li, Yikun Fu, Kaifeng Liu, Kaiyan Zhang, Dazhi Zhang, Yuqiang Li, Biqing Qi, Bowen Zhou
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) paradigms have demonstrated strong performance on reasoning-intensive tasks such as code generation. However, limited trajectory diversity often leads to diminishing returns, which constrains the achievable performance ceiling. Search-enhanced RL alleviates this issue by introducing structured exploration, which remains constrained by the single-agent policy priors. Meanwhile, leveraging multiple interacting policies can acquire more diverse exploratory signals, but existing approaches are typically decoupled from structured search. We propose \textbf{MARS$^2$} (Multi-Agent Reinforced Tree-Search Scaling), a unified RL framework in which multiple independently-optimized agents collaborate within a shared tree-structured search environment. MARS$^2$ models the search tree as a learnable multi-agent interaction environment, enabling heterogeneous agents to collaboratively generate and refine candidate solutions within a shared search topology. To support effective learning, we introduce a path-level group advantage formulation based on tree-consistent reward shaping, which facilitates effective credit assignment across complex search trajectories. Experiments on code generation benchmarks show that MARS$^2$ consistently improves performance across diverse model combinations and training settings, demonstrating the effectiveness of coupling multi-agent collaboration with tree search for enhancing reinforcement learning. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/TsinghuaC3I/MARTI.
title_zh: MARS$^2$：通过强化学习实现多智能体树搜索的规模化，用于代码生成
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）范式在代码生成等需要深度推理的任务中表现出色。然而，轨迹多样性有限常常导致收益递减，从而制约了性能的上限。搜索增强型强化学习通过引入结构化探索缓解了这一问题，但其仍受限于单智能体策略先验。另一方面，利用多个相互作用的策略可以获取更丰富的探索信号，但现有方法通常与结构化搜索解耦。我们提出 \textbf{MARS$^2$}（Multi-Agent Reinforced Tree-Search Scaling），一个统一的强化学习框架，其中多个独立优化的智能体在共享的树状搜索环境中协同工作。MARS$^2$ 将搜索树建模为可学习的多智能体交互环境，使异构智能体能够在共享的搜索拓扑结构中协同生成并优化候选解。为支持有效学习，我们引入了一种基于树一致性奖励设计的路径级分组优势形式，有助于在复杂的搜索轨迹中实现有效的信用分配。在代码生成基准上的实验表明，MARS$^2$ 在多种模型组合和训练设置下均能持续提升性能，验证了将多智能体协作与树搜索相结合对增强强化学习的有效性。我们的代码已公开，地址为 https://github.com/TsinghuaC3I/MARTI。

title: CBCL: Safe Self-Extending Agent Communication
authors: Hugo O'Connor
abstract: Agent communication languages (ACLs) enable heterogeneous agents to share knowledge and coordinate across diverse domains. This diversity demands extensibility, but expressive extension mechanisms can push the input language beyond the complexity classes where full validation is tractable. We present CBCL (Common Business Communication Language), an agent communication language that constrains all messages, including runtime language extensions, to the deterministic context-free language (DCFL) class. CBCL allows agents to define, transmit, and adopt domain-specific "dialect" extensions as first-class messages; three safety invariants (R1--R3), machine-checked in Lean 4 and enforced in a Rust reference implementation, prevent unbounded expansion, applying declared resource limits, and preserving core vocabulary. We formalize the language and its safety properties in Lean 4, implement a reference parser and dialect engine in Rust with property-based and differential tests, and extract a verified parser binary. Our results demonstrate that homoiconic protocol design, where extension definitions share the same representation as ordinary messages, can be made provably safe. As autonomous agents increasingly extend their own communication capabilities, formally bounding what they can express to each other is a precondition for oversight.
title_zh: CBCL：安全的自我扩展代理通信
abstract_zh: 代理通信语言（ACLs）使异构代理能够在不同领域间共享知识并进行协调。这种多样性要求语言具备可扩展性，但过于丰富的扩展机制可能使输入语言超出全验证在计算上可行的复杂度类别。我们提出了一种名为CBCL（通用商业通信语言）的代理通信语言，该语言将所有消息——包括运行时的语言扩展——限制在确定性上下文无关语言（DCFL）类别内。CBCL允许代理将特定领域的“方言”扩展作为一等消息进行定义、传输和采纳；三个安全不变量（R1–R3）在Lean 4中经过机器验证，并在Rust参考实现中强制执行，以防止无限扩张、应用已声明的资源限制，并保持核心词汇的完整性。我们在Lean 4中形式化了该语言及其安全属性，在Rust中实现了参考解析器与方言引擎，并通过基于属性和差异测试进行了验证，最终提取出一个经过验证的解析器二进制文件。我们的结果表明，当扩展定义与普通消息采用相同表示形式（即同像协议设计）时，这种设计可以被证明是安全的。随着自主代理越来越多地自我扩展其通信能力，对它们之间所能表达内容的形式化约束，已成为实现有效监管的前提条件。

title: Applying SHAPR in AI-Assisted Research Software Development: Lessons Learnt from Building a Share Trading System
authors: Ka Ching Chan
abstract: Generative AI is changing how research software is developed, but rapid AI-assisted development can weaken continuity, traceability, and methodological clarity. SHAPR (Solo, Human-centred, AI-assisted PRactice) was proposed as a framework for structuring AI-assisted research software development. This paper presents a documented case of applying SHAPR to the development of a modular share trading system. From the outset, the project adopted a SHAPR-informed working configuration that shaped how interaction, implementation, and documentation were organised. Across iterative development cycles, the project generated a structured evidence base including reflection notes, development cycle review notes, source-of-truth documents, contracts, quick captures, workflow notes, and evolving code artefacts. The case showed that continuous documentation updates, supported by quick capture and AI-assisted refinement, helped maintain organised and usable project knowledge throughout development. Five recurring lessons were identified: contracts stabilised AI-assisted coding, a maintained source-of-truth layer improved coherence, cycle-boundary snapshots strengthened continuity, code and documentation co-evolved through quick capture and iterative refinement, and environment setup itself contributed to knowledge generation. The case also illustrates a practical SHAPR operating configuration in which a ChatGPT Project and cycle-specific chats supported interaction, reasoning, summarisation, and coding collaboration, PyCharm supported artefact implementation, and Obsidian supported external working memory, structured documentation, reflection, continuity, and repository-oriented note organisation, while remaining consistent with SHAPR's tool-agnostic principle. The paper contributes practical guidance and good practices for researchers conducting AI-assisted research software development.
title_zh: 在人工智能辅助研究软件开发中应用SHAPR：构建股票交易系统的经验教训
abstract_zh:
生成式人工智能正在改变研究软件的开发方式，但快速的AI辅助开发可能削弱工作的连续性、可追溯性和方法学清晰度。为此，提出了SHAPR（独立、以人类为中心、AI辅助的实践）框架，用于规范AI辅助的研究软件开发流程。本文呈现了一个应用SHAPR框架开发模块化股票交易系统的案例。从项目初期开始，团队便采用基于SHAPR理念的工作配置，从而塑造了交互、实现与文档组织的方式。在多次迭代开发周期中，项目形成了结构化的证据基础，包括反思笔记、开发周期回顾记录、权威文档、合同文件、快速记录、工作流笔记以及不断演进的代码成果。该案例表明，通过快速记录和AI辅助优化持续更新文档，有助于在整个开发过程中保持项目知识的条理性和可用性。

研究总结出五个反复出现的经验教训：合同有助于稳定AI辅助编码过程；维护一个“事实来源”层级提升了整体一致性；在每个开发周期边界进行快照强化了工作连续性；代码与文档通过快速记录和迭代优化共同演进；环境配置本身也促进了知识的生成。

此外，该案例还展示了SHAPR的实际操作配置：使用ChatGPT项目及针对各周期的特定聊天对话来支持互动、推理、摘要生成与协作编码；PyCharm用于成果实现；Obsidian则作为外部工作记忆工具，支持结构化文档、反思记录、连续性维护以及面向代码仓库的笔记组织，同时始终遵循SHAPR所倡导的工具无关原则。本文为开展AI辅助研究软件开发的研究人员提供了实用指导与良好实践参考。

title: Dr.~RTL: Autonomous Agentic RTL Optimization through Tool-Grounded Self-Improvement
authors: Wenji Fang, Yao Lu, Shang Liu, Jing Wang, Ziyan Guo, Junxian He, Fengbin Tu, Zhiyao Xie
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have sparked growing interest in automatic RTL optimization for better performance, power, and area (PPA). However, existing methods are still far from realistic RTL optimization. Their evaluation settings are often unrealistic: they are tested on manually degraded, small-scale RTL designs and rely on weak open-source tools. Their optimization methods are also limited, relying on coarse design-level feedback and simple pre-defined rewriting rules. To address these limitations, we present Dr. RTL, an agentic framework for RTL timing optimization in a realistic evaluation environment, with continual self-improvement through reusable optimization skills. We establish a realistic evaluation setting with more challenging RTL designs and an industrial EDA workflow. Within this setting, Dr. RTL performs closed-loop optimization through a multi-agent framework for critical-path analysis, parallel RTL rewriting, and tool-based evaluation. We further introduce group-relative skill learning, which compares parallel RTL rewrites and distills the optimization experience into an interpretable skill library. Currently, this library contains 47 pattern--strategy entries for cross-design reuse to improve PPA and accelerate convergence, and it can continue evolving over time. Evaluated on 20 real-world RTL designs, Dr. RTL achieves average WNS/TNS improvements of 21\%/17\% with a 6\% area reduction over the industry-leading commercial synthesis tool.
title_zh: Dr. RTL：通过工具驱动的自我改进实现自主代理RTL优化
abstract_zh: 近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）的进展激发了人们对自动RTL优化以提升性能、功耗和面积（PPA）的浓厚兴趣。然而，现有方法距离实际的RTL优化仍相去甚远。其评估设置往往不切实际：通常在人工降级的小规模RTL设计上进行测试，并依赖功能较弱的开源工具；同时，其优化方法也受限于粗粒度的设计层面反馈以及简单的预定义重写规则。为解决上述局限，我们提出Dr. RTL——一种面向真实评估环境的RTL时序优化智能体框架，具备通过可复用的优化技能实现持续自我改进的能力。我们构建了一个更具挑战性的现实评估环境，包含更复杂的RTL设计及工业级EDA工作流。在此环境中，Dr. RTL通过多智能体框架实现闭环优化，涵盖关键路径分析、并行RTL重写与基于工具的评估。我们进一步引入“群体相对技能学习”机制，通过对比并行的RTL重写结果，将优化经验提炼为可解释的技能库。目前，该技能库已包含47个模式-策略条目，支持跨设计复用，有效改善PPA并加速收敛过程，且能够随时间不断演化。在20个真实世界RTL设计上的评估表明，Dr. RTL相较于业界领先的商用综合工具，平均实现了21%的WNS（最大负松弛）改善、17%的TNS（总负松弛）改善，并带来6%的面积缩减。

title: El Agente Forjador: Task-Driven Agent Generation for Quantum Simulation
authors: Zijian Zhang, Aiwei Yin, Amaan Baweja, Jiaru Bai, Ignacio Gustin, Varinia Bernales, Alán Aspuru-Guzik
abstract: AI for science promises to accelerate the discovery process. The advent of large language models (LLMs) and agentic workflows enables the expediting of a growing range of scientific tasks. However, most of the current generation of agentic systems depend on static, hand-curated toolsets that hinder adaptation to new domains and evolving libraries. We present El Agente Forjador, a multi-agent framework in which universal coding agents autonomously forge, validate, and reuse computational tools through a four-stage workflow of tool analysis, tool generation, task execution, and iterative solution evaluation. Evaluated across 24 tasks spanning quantum chemistry and quantum dynamics on five coding agent setups, we compare three operating modes: zero-shot generation of tools per task, reuse of a curriculum-built toolset, and direct problem-solving with the coding agents as the baseline. We find that our tool generation and reuse framework consistently improves accuracy over the baseline. We also show that reusing a toolset built by a stronger coding agent can reduce API cost and substantially raises the solution quality for weaker coding agents. Case studies further demonstrate that tools forged for different domains can be combined to solve hybrid tasks. Taken together, these results show that LLM-based agents can use their scientific knowledge and coding capabilities to autonomously build reusable scientific tools, pointing toward a paradigm in which agent capabilities are defined by the tasks they are designed to solve rather than by explicitly engineered implementations.
title_zh: 量子模拟任务驱动的代理生成：锻造者代理
abstract_zh: 人工智能在科学领域的应用有望加速发现进程。大型语言模型（LLMs）和智能体工作流的出现，使得越来越多的科学任务得以快速推进。然而，当前大多数智能体系统仍依赖于静态、人工精心设计的工具集，这限制了其在新领域中的适应能力以及对不断演进的软件库的响应能力。我们提出了“El Agente Forjador”——一个由多智能体构成的框架，其中通用编码智能体通过一个包含四个阶段的工作流程：工具分析、工具生成、任务执行和迭代解决方案评估，自主地锻造、验证并复用计算工具。我们在五个不同的编码智能体配置下，针对涵盖量子化学与量子动力学的24项任务进行了评估，并比较了三种运行模式：针对每项任务进行零样本工具生成、复用由课程训练构建的工具集，以及将编码智能体直接用于问题求解作为基线。结果表明，我们的工具生成与复用框架在各项任务中均持续提升了准确率。此外，我们还发现，由更强的编码智能体构建的工具集若被较弱的智能体复用，不仅能显著降低API使用成本，还能大幅提升后者的解决方案质量。案例研究进一步证明，为不同领域锻造的工具可以组合起来解决混合型任务。综上所述，这些结果表明，基于大语言模型的智能体能够利用其科学知识与编程能力，自主构建可复用的科学工具，预示着一种新的范式：智能体的能力不再由预先工程化的实现所定义，而是由其被设计解决的任务本身所决定。

title: Prompt Optimization Is a Coin Flip: Diagnosing When It Helps in Compound AI Systems
authors: Xing Zhang, Guanghui Wang, Yanwei Cui, Wei Qiu, Ziyuan Li, Bing Zhu, Peiyang He
abstract: Prompt optimization in compound AI systems is statistically indistinguishable from a coin flip: across 72 optimization runs on Claude Haiku (6 methods $\times$ 4 tasks $\times$ 3 repeats), 49% score below zero-shot; on Amazon Nova Lite, the failure rate is even higher. Yet on one task, all six methods improve over zero-shot by up to $+6.8$ points. What distinguishes success from failure? We investigate with 18,000 grid evaluations and 144 optimization runs, testing two assumptions behind end-to-end optimization tools like TextGrad and DSPy: (A) individual prompts are worth optimizing, and (B) agent prompts interact, requiring joint optimization. Interaction effects are never significant ($p > 0.52$, all $F < 1.0$), and optimization helps only when the task has exploitable output structure -- a format the model can produce but does not default to. We provide a two-stage diagnostic: an \$80 ANOVA pre-test for agent coupling, and a 10-minute headroom test that predicts whether optimization is worthwhile -- turning a coin flip into an informed decision.
title_zh: 提示优化如同掷硬币：诊断在复合人工智能系统中何时有效
abstract_zh: 在复合型AI系统中，提示优化的效果在统计上与抛硬币无异：在Claude Haiku上进行的72次优化实验（6种方法 × 4个任务 × 3次重复）中，有49%的结果低于零样本表现；而在Amazon Nova Lite上，失败率更高。然而，在某一项任务中，所有六种方法均使性能相比零样本提升最多达+6.8分。究竟是什么决定了成功与失败？我们通过18,000次网格评估和144次优化运行，对TextGrad和DSPy等端到端优化工具背后的两个假设进行了检验：(A) 单个提示值得优化；(B) 代理提示之间存在相互作用，需联合优化。结果表明，交互效应从未达到显著水平（p > 0.52，所有F值 < 1.0），而优化仅在任务具备可利用的输出结构时才有效——即模型能够生成该格式，但默认不会输出。为此，我们提出一种两阶段诊断方法：首先进行一次80美元成本的方差分析（ANOVA）预测试，以检测代理间的耦合程度；其次进行一个耗时10分钟的“余量测试”，用以预测优化是否值得投入。这一方法将原本如同掷硬币般的决策，转变为基于证据的明智选择。

title: VeriGraphi: A Multi-Agent Framework of Hierarchical RTL Generation for Large Hardware Designs
authors: Sazzadul Islam, Tasnim Tabassum, Hao Zheng
abstract: Generating synthesizable Verilog for large, hierarchical hardware designs remains a significant challenge for large language models (LLMs), which struggle to replicate the structured reasoning that human experts employ when translating complex specifications into RTL. When tasked with producing hierarchical Verilog, LLMs frequently lose context across modules, hallucinate interfaces, fabricate inter-module wiring, and fail to maintain structural coherence - failures that intensify as design complexity grows and specifications involve informal prose, figures, and tables that resist direct operationalization. To address these challenges, we present VeriGraphi, a framework that introduces a spec-anchored Knowledge Graph as the architectural substrate driving the RTL generation pipeline. VeriGraphi constructs a HDA, a structured knowledge graph that explicitly encodes module hierarchy, port-level interfaces, wiring semantics, and inter-module dependencies as first-class graph entities and relations. Built through iterative multi-agent analysis of the specification, this Knowledge Graph provides a deterministic, machine-checkable structural scaffold before code generation. Guided by the KG, a progressive coding module incrementally generates pseudo-code and synthesizable RTL while enforcing interface consistency and dependency correctness at each submodule stage. We evaluate VeriGraphi on a benchmark of three representative specification documents from the National Institute of Standards and Technology and their corresponding implementations, and we present a RV32I processor as a detailed case study to illustrate the full pipeline. The results demonstrate that VeriGraphi enables reliable hierarchical RTL generation with minimal human intervention for RISC-V, marking a significant milestone for LLM-generated hardware design while maintaining strong functional correctness.
title_zh: VeriGraphi：面向大型硬件设计的分层RTL生成多智能体框架
abstract_zh:
对于大型、分层的硬件设计，生成可综合的Verilog代码仍然是大型语言模型（LLMs）面临的重要挑战。这些模型在将复杂规格说明书转化为RTL（寄存器传输级）时，难以复现人类专家所采用的结构化推理过程。当被要求生成分层的Verilog代码时，LLMs常常在模块之间丢失上下文，虚构接口，编造模块间的连接线路，并无法保持整体结构的一致性——这些问题随着设计复杂度的增加而加剧，尤其在规格说明包含非正式的文本描述、图表和表格等难以直接操作化的内容时更为突出。

为应对上述挑战，我们提出了VeriGraphi框架，该框架引入以规格说明书为中心的知识图谱（Knowledge Graph, KG）作为驱动RTL生成流程的架构基础。VeriGraphi构建了一个硬件设计知识图谱（HDA），该图谱以结构化的方式显式编码了模块层次结构、端口级接口、布线语义以及模块间依赖关系，将其作为图中的第一类实体与关系进行表达。该知识图谱通过多智能体对规格说明书进行迭代分析而构建，能够在代码生成之前提供一个确定性的、机器可验证的结构骨架。在该知识图谱的引导下，一个渐进式编码模块逐步生成伪代码和可综合的RTL代码，同时在每个子模块阶段强制保证接口一致性与依赖关系的正确性。

我们在美国国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）提供的三份代表性规格文档及其对应实现上对VeriGraphi进行了基准测试，并以RV32I处理器作为详细案例研究，展示了整个流程的完整应用。实验结果表明，VeriGraphi能够以极小的人工干预实现可靠的RISC-V分层RTL生成，标志着LLM生成硬件设计的一个重要里程碑，同时确保了强大的功能正确性。

title: SkillDroid: Compile Once, Reuse Forever
authors: Qijia Chen, Andrea Bellucci, Zhida Sun, Giulio Jacucci
abstract: LLM-based mobile GUI agents treat every task invocation as an independent reasoning episode, requiring a full LLM inference call at each action step. This per-step dependence makes them stateless: a task completed successfully yesterday is re-derived from scratch today, with no improvement in reliability or speed. We present SkillDroid, a three-layer skill agent that compiles successful LLM-guided GUI trajectories into parameterized skill templates (sequences of UI actions with weighted element locators and typed parameter slots) and replays them on future invocations without any LLM calls. A matching cascade (regex patterns, embedding similarity, and app filtering) routes incoming instructions to stored skills, while a failure-learning layer triggers recompilation when skill reliability degrades. Over a 150-round longitudinal evaluation with systematic instruction variation and controlled perturbations, SkillDroid achieves an 85.3% success rate (23 percentage points above a stateless LLM baseline) while using 49% fewer LLM calls. The skill replay mechanism achieves a perfect 1000% success rate across 79 replay rounds at 2.4 times the speed of full LLM execution. Most critically, the system improves with use: its success rate converges upward from 87% to 91%, while the baseline degrades from 80% to 44%.
title_zh: SkillDroid：一次编译，永久复用
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的移动GUI代理将每次任务调用都视为独立的推理过程，导致每个操作步骤都需要进行完整的LLM推理调用。这种逐步依赖性使其成为无状态系统：即使昨天成功完成的任务，今天也需从头重新推导，无法在可靠性或执行速度上实现任何提升。我们提出了SkillDroid——一种三层技能代理架构，能够将成功的LLM引导式GUI操作轨迹编译为参数化的技能模板（包含加权元素定位器和类型化参数槽的UI操作序列），并在未来的任务调用中直接重放这些模板，无需任何LLM调用。通过匹配级联机制（包括正则表达式模式、嵌入相似度与应用过滤）将新指令路由至已存储的技能，同时引入故障学习层，在技能可靠性下降时触发重新编译。在包含150轮纵向评估、系统性指令变化及受控扰动的实验中，SkillDroid实现了85.3%的成功率（比无状态LLM基线高出23个百分点），同时减少了49%的LLM调用次数。其技能重放机制在79次重放轮次中达到1000%的成功率，执行速度是完整LLM执行的2.4倍。最关键的是，该系统随着使用不断优化：其成功率从87%稳步上升至91%，而基线系统则从80%持续下降至44%。

title: Schema Key Wording as an Instruction Channel in Structured Generation under Constrained Decoding
authors: Yifan Le
abstract: Constrained decoding has been widely adopted for structured generation with large language models (LLMs), ensuring that outputs satisfy predefined formats such as JSON and XML. However, existing approaches largely treat schemas as purely structural constraints and overlook the possibility that their linguistic formulation may affect model behavior. In this work, we study how instruction placement influences model performance in structured generation and show that merely changing the wording of schema keys, without modifying the prompt or model parameters, can significantly alter model performance under constrained decoding. Based on this observation, we propose to reinterpret structured generation as a multi-channel instruction problem, where instructions can be conveyed explicitly through prompts and implicitly through schema keys during decoding. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to systematically study how schema key formulation acts as an implicit instruction channel and affects model performance under constrained decoding. Experiments on multiple mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that different model families exhibit distinct sensitivities to these instruction channels: Qwen models consistently benefit from schema-level instructions, while LLaMA models rely more heavily on prompt-level guidance. We further observe non-additive interaction effects between instruction channels, showing that combining multiple channels does not always lead to further improvement. These findings suggest that schema design not only determines output structure, but also carries instruction signals, offering a new perspective on structured generation in LLMs.
title_zh: 在受限解码下的结构化生成中，模式键词作为指令通道
abstract_zh: 约束解码已被广泛应用于大语言模型（LLMs）的结构化生成任务中，以确保输出满足预定义的格式要求，如JSON和XML。然而，现有方法大多将模式（schema）视为纯粹的结构约束，忽略了其语言表述方式可能影响模型行为的可能性。在本研究中，我们探讨了指令位置如何影响模型在结构化生成中的表现，并发现仅通过改变模式键的措辞（而不修改提示或模型参数），即可显著改变模型在约束解码下的性能。基于这一观察，我们提出将结构化生成重新理解为一个多通道指令问题：在解码过程中，指令可通过提示显式传达，也可通过模式键隐式传递。据我们所知，这是首个系统性研究模式键表述作为隐式指令通道如何影响约束解码下模型性能的工作。在多个数学推理基准上的实验表明，不同模型家族对这些指令通道表现出不同的敏感性：Qwen系列模型始终从模式层级的指令中获益，而LLaMA系列模型则更依赖于提示层级的引导。此外，我们还观察到指令通道之间存在非叠加的交互效应，即同时使用多个通道并不总能带来进一步的性能提升。这些发现表明，模式设计不仅决定输出结构，还承载着指令信号，为大语言模型中的结构化生成提供了新的视角。

title: Bounded Autonomy for Enterprise AI: Typed Action Contracts and Consumer-Side Execution
authors: Sarmad Sohail, Ghufran Haider
abstract: Large language models are increasingly used as natural-language interfaces to enterprise software, but their direct use as system operators remains unsafe. Model errors can propagate into unauthorized actions, malformed requests, cross-workspace execution, and other costly failures. We argue this is primarily an execution architecture problem. We present a bounded-autonomy architecture in which language models may interpret intent and propose actions, but all executable behavior is constrained by typed action contracts, permission-aware capability exposure, scoped context, validation before side effects, consumer-side execution boundaries, and optional human approval. The enterprise application remains the source of truth for business logic and authorization, while the orchestration engine operates over an explicit published actions manifest. We evaluate the architecture in a deployed multi-tenant enterprise application across three conditions: manual operation, unconstrained AI with safety layers disabled, and full bounded autonomy. Across 25 scenario trials spanning seven failure families, the bounded-autonomy system completed 23 of 25 tasks with zero unsafe executions, while the unconstrained configuration completed only 17 of 25. Two wrong-entity mutations escaped all consumer-contributed layers; only disambiguation and confirmation mechanisms intercept this class. Both AI conditions delivered 13-18x speedup over manual operation. Critically, removing safety layers made the system less useful: structured validation feedback guided the model to correct outcomes in fewer turns, while the unconstrained system hallucinated success. Several safety properties are structurally enforced by code and intercepted all targeted violations regardless of model output. The result is a practical, deployed architecture for making imperfect language models operationally useful in enterprise systems.
title_zh: 企业人工智能的有限自主性：类型化动作契约与消费者端执行
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型正越来越多地被用作企业软件的自然语言接口，但直接将其作为系统操作员使用仍存在安全隐患。模型错误可能引发未经授权的操作、格式错误的请求、跨工作区执行以及其他代价高昂的故障。我们认为，这本质上是一个执行架构问题。本文提出一种“有限自主性”架构：语言模型可解析用户意图并提出操作建议，但所有可执行行为均受到类型化操作契约、权限感知的能力暴露、作用域受限的上下文、副作用前的验证、消费者端执行边界以及可选的人工审批等多重约束。企业应用始终保持业务逻辑和授权的权威性，而编排引擎则基于显式发布的操作清单进行运作。

我们在一个部署于多租户企业应用中的系统上评估了该架构，共设置三种条件：手动操作、无约束AI（安全层禁用）以及完整的有限自主模式。在涵盖七类故障的25个场景测试中，有限自主系统成功完成23项任务，且未发生任何不安全执行；而无约束配置仅完成17项。有两个错误实体的变更绕过了所有由消费者贡献的安全层，只有通过消歧和确认机制才能拦截此类问题。两种AI配置相比手动操作均实现了13至18倍的速度提升。关键的是，移除安全层反而降低了系统的实用性：结构化的验证反馈能够引导模型在更少轮次内达成正确结果，而无约束系统则会虚构成功。多个安全属性通过代码实现结构性强制，无论模型输出如何，均能拦截所有目标违规行为。

最终，我们构建出一种实用且已投入生产的架构，使不完美的语言模型能够在企业系统中实现安全、可靠的运营应用。

title: Structural Dependency Analysis for Masked NTT Hardware: Scalable Pre-Silicon Verification of Post-Quantum Cryptographic Accelerators
authors: Ray Iskander, Khaled Kirah
abstract: Post-quantum cryptographic accelerators require side-channel resistance evidence for FIPS 140-3 certification. However, exact masking-verification tools scale only to gadgets of a few thousand cells. We present a four-stage verification hierarchy, D0/D1 structural dependency analysis, fresh-mask refinement, Boolean Single-Authentication Distance Checking (SADC), and arithmetic SADC, that extends sound first-order masking verification to production arithmetic modules. Applied to the 1.17-million-cell Adams Bridge ML-DSA/ML-KEM accelerator, structural analysis completes in seconds across all 30 masked submodules. A multi-cycle extension (MC-D1) reclassifies 12 modules from structurally clean to structurally flagged. On the 5,543-cell ML-KEM Barrett reduction module, the pipeline machine-verifies 198 of 363 structurally flagged wires (54.5%) as first-order secure, reports 165 as candidate insecure for designer triage (a sound upper bound), and leaves 0 indeterminate. Every verdict is cross validated by Z3 and CVC5 with 0 disagreements across 363 wires. The result narrows manual review from hundreds of structural flags to 165 actionable candidates with mathematical certificates, enabling pre-silicon side-channel evidence generation on production ML-KEM hardware.
title_zh: 基于掩码NTT硬件的结构依赖性分析：后量子密码加速器的可扩展预硅验证
abstract_zh: 后量子密码加速器在申请FIPS 140-3认证时，需要提供抗侧信道攻击的证明。然而，现有的精确掩码验证工具仅能处理包含数千个单元的小型电路模块。本文提出了一种四阶段验证体系：D0/D1结构依赖性分析、新型掩码精炼、布尔单认证距离检查（SADC）以及算术SADC，将可靠的首阶掩码验证能力扩展至生产级算术模块。该方法应用于包含117万逻辑单元的Adams Bridge ML-DSA/ML-KEM加速器时，结构分析可在数秒内完成全部30个掩码子模块的检测。通过引入多周期扩展（MC-D1），有12个模块从原本“结构无风险”被重新分类为“结构存在风险”。针对5,543个逻辑单元的ML-KEM Barrett约减模块，该流水线化验证机制自动识别出363条结构上存在风险的信号线中198条（54.5%）为首阶安全，报告其余165条为潜在不安全候选，供设计者进一步排查（提供可靠上界），并确保无任何不确定结果。所有判断均经Z3与CVC5双重交叉验证，363条信号线中未出现任何分歧。最终，将人工审查范围从数百个结构风险标记缩减至165个可操作的高置信度候选对象，并附带数学证明证书，从而实现了在芯片流片前即生成生产级ML-KEM硬件的抗侧信道证据。

title: QLLVM: A Scalable Quantum-Classical Co-Compilation Framework based on LLVM
authors: Yu Zhu, Qiming Du, Yuqiong Jin, Woji He, Hang Lian, Xin Zhou, Jinchen Xu, Zheng Shan
abstract: To address the urgent need in the NISQ era for high-performance, scalable quantum compilers and to advance the integration of classical and quantum computing, we present QLLVM, an advanced Quantum-Classical co-compilation framework built on LLVM. To our knowledge, QLLVM delivers an end-to-end, LLVM-based compilation workflow that unifies the build of classical high-performance programs, including CUDA, MPI, and C++, together with quantum programs into a single executable. For quantum program compilation, QLLVM adopts a three-stage design: high-level optimizations are implemented in the MLIR Quantum dialect and then lowered to QIR, an LLVM IR-based representation, for low-level optimization and hardware mapping. Its extensible architecture and seamless interoperability with classical high-performance computing provide an efficient, flexible, industrial-grade compilation infrastructure for future quantum software development. Experimental results show that, on the MQTBench benchmark suite, QLLVM reduces circuit depth and gate counts compared with state-of-the-art compilers and demonstrates clear advantages in compiling hybrid classical-quantum programs.
title_zh: QLLVM：一种基于LLVM的可扩展量子-经典协同编译框架
abstract_zh: 为应对NISQ时代对高性能、可扩展量子编译器的迫切需求，并推动经典计算与量子计算的深度融合，我们提出QLLVM——一种基于LLVM的先进量子-经典协同编译框架。据我们所知，QLLVM实现了端到端、基于LLVM的编译流程，将经典高性能程序（包括CUDA、MPI和C++）与量子程序的构建统一整合到单一可执行文件中。在量子程序编译方面，QLLVM采用三阶段设计：高层优化在MLIR量子方言中实现，随后降级至QIR（基于LLVM IR的表示形式），以进行低层优化和硬件映射。其可扩展的架构以及与经典高性能计算的无缝互操作性，为未来量子软件开发提供了高效、灵活且具备工业级标准的编译基础设施。实验结果表明，在MQTBench基准测试套件上，QLLVM相较于当前最先进的编译器，显著降低了电路深度和门数量，并在编译混合经典-量子程序方面展现出明显优势。

title: Automatic Enhancement of Cell Models to Enable Conditional Stuck-at Fault Simulation and Pattern Generation
authors: Reza Khoshzaban, Nikolaos I. Deligiannis, Iacopo Guglielminetti, Michelangelo Grosso, Riccardo Cantoro
abstract: Automatic test pattern generation (ATPG) and fault simulation methods for stuck-at faults (SAFs) cannot accurately capture the behavior of conditional stuck-at faults (CSAFs), which manifest only under specific input combinations. To manage such conditional faults using conventional stuck-at fault simulators and test pattern generators, we propose a methodology that embeds conditional fault behavior directly into the logic description of standard cells. The method operates on conditional stuck-at fault models, which define the output variations of a cell in the presence of specific conditional faults for all input stimuli. Each cell model is translated into a Boolean equation that represent the internal logic conditions required to activate the associated faults. This equation is then integrated into the functional description of the standard cell, without modifying its primary inputs, outputs, or intended behavior. As a result, the augmented standard cell library can reproduce the effects of conditional stuck-at faults while remaining compatible with existing fault simulation and ATPG tools. The proposed method incurs negligible application time, and experiments on several benchmark circuits and cores demonstrate reasonable overhead in test pattern count and CPU time.
title_zh: 自动增强细胞模型以实现条件性粘连故障仿真与测试矢量生成
abstract_zh: 自动测试图案生成（ATPG）和针对固定故障（SAF）的故障仿真方法无法准确捕捉条件固定故障（CSAF）的行为，因为这类故障仅在特定输入组合下才会显现。为利用传统固定故障仿真器和测试图案生成工具来处理此类条件故障，我们提出了一种将条件故障行为直接嵌入标准单元逻辑描述中的方法。该方法基于条件固定故障模型，这些模型定义了在特定条件故障存在时，对所有输入激励下单元输出的变化情况。每个单元模型被转换为一个布尔方程，用以表示激活相关故障所需的内部逻辑条件。随后，该方程被整合到标准单元的功能描述中，而无需修改其主要输入、输出或预期功能。结果，经过增强的标准单元库能够在保持与现有故障仿真和ATPG工具兼容性的同时，重现条件固定故障的影响。所提方法的应用时间开销可忽略不计，对多个基准电路和核心进行的实验表明，其在测试图案数量和CPU时间方面均表现出合理的开销。

title: Smart Contract Implementation in Web-Based E-Voting Systems: Blockchain Security and Transparency Analysis
authors: Bryan Liem, Vincent Nicholas Hie, Zulfany Erlisa Rasjid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Traditional e-voting systems face critical challenges that undermine their security, transparency, and integrity, including data manipulation and vote fraud. This study addresses these issues by proposing a novel, blockchain based solution designed to enforce election rules securely without centralized control. We design and implement a web based e-voting prototype using smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain, leveraging a decentralized stack that integrates React, MetaMask, Ethers.js, and Hardhat. While previous research has explored blockchain for e-voting, many studies focus on architecture but lack a comprehensive security analysis of the underlying smart contracts. Our key contribution lies in a rigorous, multi-faceted security analysis that validates the system's robustness. We employed static analysis with Slither, which successfully identified and mitigated specific timestamp dependency vulnerabilities, ensuring code quality. Furthermore, we achieved a 100% statement, function, and line coverage through unit testing by executing 25 test cases in under one second, and conducted extensive fuzz testing with Echidna, which reported no invariant violations. The results confirm the system's ability to ensure voter anonymity through cryptographic hashing, prevent double-voting, and maintain the immutability of the ballot ledger, directly addressing the vulnerabilities found in conventional systems. This research not only provides a practical, verifiable solution for secure e-voting but also establishes a robust methodology for auditing the security of smart contracts in high-stakes applications.
title_zh: 基于区块链的Web电子投票系统中智能合约的实现：安全与透明性分析
abstract_zh: 传统电子投票系统面临诸多严峻挑战，严重威胁其安全性、透明性和完整性，包括数据篡改和选票欺诈等问题。本研究提出了一种基于区块链的创新解决方案，旨在在无需中心化控制的情况下安全地执行选举规则。我们设计并实现了一个基于Web的电子投票原型系统，利用以太坊区块链上的智能合约，构建于一个去中心化技术栈之上，集成了React、MetaMask、Ethers.js和Hardhat等工具。尽管先前的研究已探索将区块链应用于电子投票，但多数工作仅关注系统架构，缺乏对底层智能合约的全面安全分析。本研究的核心贡献在于开展了一项严谨且多维度的安全分析，验证了系统的鲁棒性。我们采用Slither进行静态分析，成功识别并修复了特定的时间戳依赖漏洞，保障了代码质量。此外，通过在不到一秒内执行25个测试用例，实现了100%的语句、函数和行覆盖率的单元测试，并使用Echidna进行了广泛的模糊测试，未发现任何不变量违规情况。结果表明，该系统能够通过密码学哈希确保选民匿名性，防止重复投票，并保持选票记录的不可篡改性，有效应对传统系统中的各类漏洞。本研究不仅为安全电子投票提供了一个可实践、可验证的解决方案，还建立了一套适用于高风险应用场景中智能合约安全审计的可靠方法论。

title: Software Diversity in Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
authors: Kush Dave, Ian G. Harris
abstract: Resource-constrained embedded devices often lack the security measures against Return-Oriented Programming (ROP) attacks, like Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR), due to overhead restrictions and device constraints. If a malicious actor were to get hold of this firmware, reverse engineer it and craft a ROP attack for a device and the firmware, all similar devices would immediately become vulnerable to this attack. Moreover, it has been shown that compared to general purpose devices, IoT and embedded devices are far slower to receive the patch for vulnerabilities, leaving many of them still vulnerable up to multiple months even after a patch has been pushed out. In this paper, we present a tool to generate logically diverse, but functionally equivalent firmware for resource constrained embedded devices. Our system is context aware and prevents the uncertain performance or large overheads usually associated with diversifying software due to addition of extra instructions. Our security analysis shows that by diversifying firmware, we are able to generate variants that are secure against generalized attacks that involve using ROP gadgets.
title_zh: 资源受限嵌入式系统中的软件多样性
abstract_zh: 资源受限的嵌入式设备通常由于开销限制和设备本身的约束，缺乏针对返回导向编程（Return-Oriented Programming, ROP）攻击的安全措施，例如地址空间布局随机化（Address Space Layout Randomization, ASLR）。如果恶意攻击者获取了该固件，对其进行逆向工程并为特定设备及固件构造ROP攻击，那么所有类似设备将立即面临此攻击的威胁。此外，已有研究表明，与通用设备相比，物联网（IoT）和嵌入式设备在漏洞修补方面响应速度远为缓慢，即使补丁已发布，许多设备仍可能在数月内持续处于易受攻击状态。本文提出了一种工具，用于为资源受限的嵌入式设备生成逻辑上多样但功能等价的固件变体。我们的系统具备上下文感知能力，能够有效避免传统软件多样化技术中常见的性能不确定性和高开销问题，这些问题通常源于额外指令的引入。安全分析表明，通过固件多样化，我们能够生成对涉及使用ROP小工具的通用攻击具有抵御能力的固件变体。

title: Evaluating Code Readability in Java-to-Kotlin Migration Using Large Language Models and Static Analysis
authors: Nirel Marie M. Ibarra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of large language models (LLMs) as quantitative evaluators of code readability in industrial-scale Java-to-Kotlin migrations. Focusing on a production Android MVVM repository, the research deploys a cross-validated methodological framework using ChatGPT-5 and Gemini-2.5 Pro, triangulated against established static analysis metrics. By systematically comparing legacy Java and refactored Kotlin code through multi-run LLM scoring and structural complexity measures, the study demonstrates that Kotlin migration yields substantial improvements in cognitive readability. The framework also achieves strong reliability through agreement between models and statistical controls. Practical implications include using LLM-driven analytics in continuous integration pipelines to monitor maintainability, guide refactoring, and reduce technical debt. The findings support an objective and scalable approach to assessing code quality and readability, providing a blueprint for modern software analytics during language and architectural transitions.
title_zh: 使用大型语言模型与静态分析评估Java到Kotlin迁移中的代码可读性
abstract_zh: 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）作为工业级Java至Kotlin迁移项目中代码可读性定量评估工具的有效性。研究聚焦于一个生产环境下的Android MVVM代码库，采用交叉验证的方法论框架，利用ChatGPT-5与Gemini-2.5 Pro进行评估，并与既有的静态分析指标进行三角验证。通过多轮次的LLM评分及结构复杂度测量，系统比较了原有的Java代码与重构后的Kotlin代码，结果表明Kotlin迁移显著提升了代码的认知可读性。该框架还通过模型间的一致性以及统计控制手段，实现了较高的可靠性。实际应用意义在于，可将基于LLM的分析工具集成到持续集成流水线中，用于监控代码可维护性、指导重构工作并降低技术债务。研究结果支持了一种客观且可扩展的代码质量与可读性评估方法，为语言和架构转型过程中的现代软件分析提供了可复制的实践范式。

title: Case Study: The High-Level Synthesis-Based Firmware Upgrade for the Digital Trigger Back-End System of the ATLAS Liquid Argon Calorimeter
authors: Melissa S. Aguiar, Dabson F. dos Santos, Adriana Milic, Diogo A. Cardinot, Marcos V. S. Oliveira, Luciano M. A. Filho, Gustavo B. Libotte, Bernardo S. Peralva
abstract: The trigger and data acquisition systems of the LHC experiments rely on reconfigurable architectures based on FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Arrays) to accommodate design updates and increasing of the detectors complexity. However, this flexibility comes at the cost of longer development and validation cycles, particularly for large real-time systems operating under strict latency and throughput constraints. This work presents the HLS-based firmware redesign of the back-end system from the ATLAS Liquid Argon Calorimeter, where the introduction of a parallel switch matrix on REMAP block significantly simplifies the architecture and reduces the use of firmware resources. The proposed methodology allows systematic design exploration without modifying the source code, shortens verification time, and provides a more efficient workflow for creating and maintaining large-scale FPGA firmware in modern trigger systems.
title_zh: 案例研究：基于高层次综合的ATLAS液态氩量能器数字触发后端系统的固件升级
abstract_zh: 大型强子对撞机（LHC）实验中的触发与数据采集系统依赖基于现场可编程门阵列（FPGA）的可重构架构，以适应设计更新及探测器复杂性的不断提升。然而，这种灵活性也带来了开发和验证周期延长的问题，尤其是在严格限制延迟和吞吐量的大规模实时系统中尤为明显。本文提出了一种基于高层次综合（HLS）的固件重设计方法，应用于ATLAS液氩量能器后端系统的重构。通过在REMAP模块中引入并行开关矩阵，显著简化了系统架构，并减少了固件资源的使用。所提出的这一方法能够在不修改源代码的前提下实现系统性设计探索，缩短验证时间，并为现代触发系统中大规模FPGA固件的设计与维护提供更高效的流程。

title: Cybersecurity Compliance for Operational Technology using Large Language Models
authors: Regan Bolton, Mohammadreza Sheikhfathollahi, Simon Parkinson, Dan Basher, Howard Parkinson
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Operational Technology Cybersecurity (OTCS) continues to be a dominant challenge for critical infrastructure such as rail. As these systems are vulnerable to malicious attacks, effective documentation and development processes are essential to perform compliance assessments and proactively protect systems. In addition to maintaining compliance, it is also necessary to periodically evaluate the design documentation to account for the evolution of cybersecurity standards and threats. In this paper, a system is proposed that uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and multi-stage retrieval to enhance the compliance verification process. Initially, a basic architecture named baseline compliance architecture (BCA) is evaluated to answer OTCS compliance queries. After this, the extended approach is defined, which is called parallel compliance architecture (PCA). The performance of OpenAI-gpt-4o and Claude-3.5-haiku models is compared when used in the pipeline against the relative performance of the BCA. A discussion of their responses is provided and, through empirical evaluation, it is observed that PCA can score 0.1 points higher in correctness and 0.55 points higher in reasoning than BCA.
title_zh: 使用大型语言模型实现工业技术的网络安全合规
abstract_zh: 工业控制系统网络安全（OTCS）对于铁路等关键基础设施而言，依然是一个主要挑战。由于这些系统易受恶意攻击，因此建立有效的文档编制与开发流程至关重要，以支持合规性评估并主动保护系统安全。除了确保持续合规外，还需定期审查设计文档，以应对网络安全标准和威胁形势的不断演变。本文提出一种新系统，利用大语言模型（LLMs）与多阶段检索技术，提升合规性验证的效率与准确性。首先，评估了一种基础架构——“基准合规架构”（Baseline Compliance Architecture, BCA），用于回答OTCS合规性相关问题。随后，提出了扩展方案，即“并行合规架构”（Parallel Compliance Architecture, PCA）。在该架构中，对比分析了OpenAI的gpt-4o模型与Claude-3.5-haiku模型在处理任务时的表现，并与BCA进行了比较。通过对模型响应的讨论及实证评估发现，PCA在正确性方面比BCA高出0.1分，在推理能力方面则高出0.55分。

title: How Secure is a High-Performance RISC-V Core? A Spectre V1 Case Study on XiangShan Open-Source CPU
authors: Rey Paraula, Roberto Petenzi, Alessandro Bertani, Davide Conficconi
publish: Proceedings of the 19th European Workshop on Systems Security
abstract: None
title_zh: 高性能RISC-V核心的安全性如何？基于香山开源CPU的Spectre V1案例研究

title: Baseline Design Method of GB-TomoArcSAR Based on Coding Optimization
authors: Weiming Tian, Kai Yan, Yunkai Deng, Xin Xie, Yuanhao Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Due to the limitation of lacking elevation resolution, ground-based interferometric radar suffers from serious layover problem when applied to building deformation monitoring. Ground-based tomographic arc-scanning synthetic aperture ra dar (GB-TomoArcSAR) enables tomography by controlling the antenna to scan multiple times in the horizontal plane of different elevation angles to form an arc-shaped synthetic aperture in the elevation direction. However, when GB-TomoArcSAR uses a baseline with uniformly distributed elevation angles, the excessive number of baseline samples leads to the low efficiency of tomog raphy. To address this issue, this paper proposes a tomographic baseline design method based on coding optimization (TBDCO). Firstly, the sampling positions in the elevation direction are bina ry coded. Then, with the elevation resolution and peak side-lobe ratio (PSLR) of the antenna pointing pattern satisfying specific constraints, a quality evaluation model of the tomographic base line is constructed by weighted summation of the sampling num ber and the Cramer Rao bound (CRB) estimation accuracy in the elevation direction. Finally, the genetic algorithm is employed to iteratively solve the optimal coding to achieve sparse design of the tomographic baseline. Validation using both simulated and measured data demonstrates that the proposed method can re duce the number of elevation samples to 42% of the original before optimization while maintaining tomographic performance, substantially shortening the time for collecting measured data.
title_zh: 基于编码优化的GB-TomoArcSAR基线设计方法
abstract_zh: 由于缺乏高程分辨率，地基干涉雷达在进行建筑物形变监测时存在严重的叠掩问题。地基层析弧扫描合成孔径雷达（GB-TomoArcSAR）通过控制天线在不同仰角的水平平面内多次扫描，在高程方向形成弧形合成孔径，从而实现层析成像。然而，当GB-TomoArcSAR采用均匀分布仰角的基线时，基线样本数量过多，导致层析效率低下。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种基于编码优化的层析基线设计方法（TBDCO）。首先，对高程方向的采样位置进行二进制编码；随后，在满足天线波束指向图高程分辨率和峰值旁瓣比（PSLR）特定约束的条件下，通过加权求和采样点数与高程方向Cramer-Rao界（CRB）估计精度，构建层析基线的质量评估模型；最后，利用遗传算法迭代求解最优编码，实现层析基线的稀疏化设计。通过仿真数据和实测数据验证表明，所提方法可在保持层析性能的前提下，将高程采样点数量减少至优化前的42%，显著缩短实测数据采集时间。

title: Enhancing Ethereum Smart Contract Security: A Novel Slither-Based Static Analysis Framework
authors: Nasrin Nemati, Nasr Abosata, Usman Butt
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Blockchain technology is evolving rapidly, bringing significant opportunities as well as complex challenges, particularly in the area of smart contract security This paper addresses these challenges by presenting an enhanced version of Slither, a static analysis framework, specifically designed to improve vulnerability detection in Ethereum-based smart contracts. Our contributions target critical vulnerabilities of reentrancy, unchecked call return values, and access control issues, which pose serious threat to decentralized apps (dApps). The enhanced Slither tool incorporates advanced detection methodologies, including refined control-flow analysis, sophisticated pattern recognition, and comprehensive visibility checks. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that this proposed tool significantly improves accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score across various metrics, while also effectively reducing false positives. This advanced model not only strengthens the security posture of smart contracts but also contributes to fostering a more secure and reliable blockchain ecosystem.
title_zh: 提升以太坊智能合约安全性的新型基于Slither的静态分析框架
abstract_zh: 区块链技术正在迅速发展，带来了巨大的机遇，同时也伴随着复杂的挑战，尤其是在智能合约安全领域。本文针对这些挑战，提出了一种改进版的Slither工具——一种静态分析框架，专门用于提升以太坊智能合约中的漏洞检测能力。我们的工作重点解决重入攻击、未检查调用返回值以及权限控制问题等关键漏洞，这些问题对去中心化应用（dApps）构成了严重威胁。所提出的增强型Slither工具集成了先进的检测方法，包括优化的控制流分析、复杂的模式识别以及全面的可见性检查。实验评估表明，该工具在多个指标上显著提升了检测的准确性、精确率、召回率和F1分数，同时有效降低了误报率。这一先进模型不仅增强了智能合约的安全性，也为构建更加安全可靠的区块链生态系统做出了重要贡献。

title: Robust and Size-Optimized Error Handling in Rust for Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
authors: Philipp Schloyer, Dominik Merli, Bernhard Bauer

abstract: None
